title: Renewable Natural Gas Quarterly Trend Sheet - Q3 2025
content:
North American RNG Output Rises 9% YoY in Q3 2025
North American RNG production is expected to reach 33.36 million MMBtu in Q3 2025, up 9% from Q3 2024â€™s 30.62 million MMBtu. The US contribution is estimated at 30.28 million MMBtu (90.7%), while Canada adds 3.08 million MMBtu. Landfill continues to dominate as feedstock, accounting for 20.74 million MMBtu (62.17%), followed by agricultural waste at 8.19 million MMBtu (24.5%). Transportation remains the largest RNG consumer, but growth is slowing as the RNG share in natural gas-powered vehicle fleets reaches saturation and gas fleet expansion slows, while residential and commercial RNG demand is expected to grow rapidly as state blending mandates and utilitiesâ€™ voluntary targets stimulate uptake. Policy support is strengthening, with Californiaâ€™s SB 1440 mandating 12.2% RNG by 2030, Oregonâ€™s SB 98 setting voluntary targets of up to 30% by 2050, and Nevadaâ€™s SB 154 requiring 3% by 2035.
author: Mohammed Saaim Akram
